diagrams.description: Cognitive Psychology is the scientific study of mental processes such as perception, attention, memory, language, and reasoning. This flowchart illustrates its framework: starting from the ultimate goal of understanding how the mind processes information and guides behavior, it integrates core mental domains (perception and attention, memory systems, language and comprehension, reasoning and decision making, problem solving). These are investigated through converging methods including behavioral experiments, cognitive neuroscience (fMRI, EEG), and computational modeling. Major theoretical perspectives—information processing theory, connectionism, and embodied cognition—shape the interpretation of findings. The insights produced apply to education, artificial intelligence, clinical interventions, and human‑computer interaction.
